name: BACKPORCH COFFEE (NEWPORT)
address: 1052 NW NEWPORT AVE STE 103, BEND, OR 97701

fr.recommendations: Enhancing the visibility and accessibility of multiple access points on the property may facilitate the movement and deployment of emergency personnel. Ensuring that the vehicle parking area is organized to allow for unobstructed entry and exit routes could support quicker response times and efficient staging of emergency vehicles. The landscaped area should be maintained in a manner that preserves clear sightlines and access for responders, thereby aiding in navigation and operational efforts. Given the limited access to the rear of the property, identifying or designating clear travel paths within the existing sidewalk network could further assist responders in reaching all areas swiftly. By concentrating on these modifications, the property owner may enhance overall emergency operations coordination and effectiveness.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the defensible space by increasing the separation between vegetation and the structure could improve property preparedness. Managing scattered trees to ensure adequate spacing and reducing the continuity of combustible materials adjacent to the building may further support defensible space. Prioritizing vegetation management in the immediate vicinity of the structure could reduce potential exposure from radiant heat and direct flame contact. Maintenance of this defensible space, coupled with consideration of the arrangement and density of trees, can help create more effective fuel breaks, thereby reducing opportunities for fire spread. This ongoing vegetation management and monitoring can contribute to long-term wildfire resilience and support both self-defense and firefighter suppression efforts.
